steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice “most effectively contrasts” the two moons’ orbits. Contrast requires showing a difference. The notes give one clear difference: Oberon takes 13.46 days per orbit; Miranda takes only 1.41 days.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Miranda completes its orbit of Uranus many times faster than Oberon does.: directly contrasts the two moons using the orbital-period data. ✓
  • Choice B — Kepler's three laws of planetary motion describe the orbits of planets and natural satellites, including those of Oberon and Miranda.: generalizes about orbital laws without comparing the two moons. ✗
  • Choice C — Oberon's orbit of Uranus is elliptical in shape and takes, on average, 13.46 Earth days, while Miranda's orbit of Uranus is circular in shape and takes, on average, 1.41 Earth days.: invents orbital shapes that the notes do not mention. ✗
  • Choice D — Uranus's moons, specifically Oberon and Miranda, orbit their planet in the same way the planets orbit the Sun.: generalizes about laws without comparing the two moons. ✗
That matches Choice A — Miranda completes its orbit of Uranus many times faster than Oberon does..
